session.user:
Verify the following requirements for the cmux skill improvement plan:
1. Check that `tree` command is NOT present in SKILL.md (it was supposed to be removed) 2. Check that `references/cmux-reference.md` covers: browser, tmux compat, windows, panels, tabs, sidebar 3. Check that SKILL.md has "When to Load the Reference" section 4. Check that SKILL.md mentions `--snapshot-after`, `claude-hook`, and `wait-for`
Read these files: - /home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/skills/cmux/SKILL.md - /home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/skills/cmux/references/cmux-reference.md
Report what you find for each requirement (pass/fail).
session.assistant: I'll verify all four requirements by reading the two files you specified.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: cmux 3→description: This skill should be used when working with cmux, the terminal multiplexer application. Use when users mention cmux commands, workspace management, terminal pane operations, or cmux integration with erk. Essential for understanding cmux's workspace model, CLI commands, and scripting patterns. 4→--- 5→ 6→# cmux - Terminal Multiplexer 7→ 8→## Overview 9→ 10→**cmux** is a native macOS terminal application (Swift/AppKit) that provides terminal multiplexing with workspaces, panes, and splits. It uses Ghostty's rendering engine (libghostty) for GPU-accelerated terminal output. The CLI communicates with the running app via a Unix domain socket at `/tmp/cmux.sock`. 11→ 12→Think of cmux as "tmux reimagined as a native macOS app" -- it has workspaces (like tmux windows), panes (like tmux panes), and surfaces (individual terminal or browser instances within panes), all controllable via a rich CLI. 13→ 14→## When to Load the Reference 15→ 16→This skill covers the mental model, common commands, gotchas, and erk integration. Load `references/cmux-reference.md` when you need: 17→ 18→- **Browser automation** syntax (40+ subcommands: navigation, forms, cookies, storage, console, waiting, dialogs, downloads, state) 19→- **Window management** (`list-windows`, `new-window`, `focus-window`, `close-window`, `move-workspace-to-window`) 20→- **Surface management** (`move-surface`, `reorder-surface`, `drag-surface-to-split`, `surface-health`) 21→- **Panel/Tab** commands (`list-panels`, `focus-panel`, `tab-action`, `rename-tab`) 22→- **Sidebar metadata** (`clear-status`, `list-status`, `clear-progress`, `clear-log`, `list-log`, `sidebar-state`) 23→- **Full tmux compat** (20+ commands: `pipe-pane`, `wait-for`, `copy-mode`, `set-hook`, `bind-key`, `popup`, etc.) 24→- **Utility/diagnostic** (`ping`, `capabilities`, `claude-hook`, `set-app-focus`) 25→- **Workflow recipes** (browser automation, layout scripting, notification workflows) 26→ 27→## Core Concepts 28→ 29→### Object Hierarchy 30→
... [230 lines omitted] ...

1→# cmux Complete Command Reference 2→ 3→Comprehensive reference for all cmux CLI commands. Load this when you need exact syntax for less-common commands. For mental model, common patterns, and gotchas, see the main `SKILL.md`. 4→ 5→## Global Flags 6→ 7→Available on all commands: 8→ 9→``` 10→--socket PATH Override socket path (default: /tmp/cmux.sock) 11→--window WINDOW Target specific window 12→--password PASSWORD Socket auth password 13→--json Output as JSON 14→--id-format refs|uuids|both Control ID format in output 15→--version, -v Show version 16→--help, -h Show help 17→``` 18→ 19→## Workspace Commands 20→ 21→| Command | Syntax | Description | 22→|---------|--------|-------------| 23→| `new-workspace` | `cmux new-workspace [--command <text>]` | Create workspace. `--command` auto-appends `\n`. Returns `OK <uuid>` | 24→| `list-workspaces` | `cmux list-workspaces` | List all workspaces | 25→| `select-workspace` | `cmux select-workspace --workspace <ref>` | Switch to workspace | 26→| `rename-workspace` | `cmux rename-workspace [--workspace <ref>] <title>` | Rename workspace | 27→| `close-workspace` | `cmux close-workspace --workspace <ref>` | Close workspace | 28→| `current-workspace` | `cmux current-workspace` | Print current workspace ID | 29→| `reorder-workspace` | `cmux reorder-workspace --workspace <ref> --position <n>` | Reorder workspace in sidebar | 30→
... [390 lines omitted] ...
225→### Console and Errors 231→| `browser errors list` | `cmux browser errors list [--surface <ref>]` | List JavaScript errors | 232→| `browser errors clear` | `cmux browser errors clear [--surface <ref>]` | Clear error list |
session.assistant:
## Verification Report: cmux Skill Improvement Plan
Based on my analysis of the two files, here are the results for each requirement:
### Requirement 1: Check that `tree` command is NOT present in SKILL.md **PASS** - I searched through the entire SKILL.md file and found no mention of a `tree` command. The command was successfully removed.
### Requirement 2: Check that `references/cmux-reference.md` covers: browser, tmux compat, windows, panels, tabs, sidebar **PASS** - The reference file comprehensively covers all six areas: - **Browser**: Lines 98-268 (extensive browser automation subsystem with 40+ commands) - **tmux compat**: Lines 269-336 (tmux Compatibility Layer section) - **Windows**: Lines 31-40 (Window Management section with list-windows, new-window, focus-window, close-window, move-workspace-to-window) - **Panels**: Lines 43-52 (Pane Commands section includes list-panels, focus-panel, send-panel, send-key-panel) - **Tabs**: Lines 76-81 (Tab Commands section with tab-action, rename-tab) - **Sidebar**: Lines 83-96 (Notification/Status/Sidebar Commands section with clear-status, list-status, clear-progress, clear-log, list-log, sidebar-state)
### Requirement 3: Check that SKILL.md has "When to Load the Reference" section **PASS** - The section exists at lines 14-25 in SKILL.md, titled "When to Load the Reference". It clearly documents when to load the reference file and lists all the major topic areas.
### Requirement 4: Check that SKILL.md mentions `--snapshot-after`, `claude-hook`, and `wait-for` **PASS** - All three are mentioned: - **`--snapshot-after`**: Line 110 - "Most interaction commands accept this flag to automatically capture a snapshot after the action" - **`claude-hook`**: Line 119 - Listed as "Claude Code integration hook" - **`wait-for`**: Line 118 - Listed as "Inter-process synchronization (tmux compat)"
All four requirements are met successfully.
